themes.name: Sun protection = habits, not products
themes.receipt: 6 breakouts · 3 superviral · 7 ♥ magnets · 12M+ views · 'sun shirt' script hit on 2 creators

videos.author: beautydermmd
videos.caption: Not her serum. Not her skincare. HER HABITS. 👀 Sun protection is elite behavior. UPF clothing. Wide-brim hat. Sunglasses. Shade. This is wha

videos.author: drdrayzday
videos.caption: Diaper rash cream is not sunscreen. Always look for sunscreens that are labeled broad spectrum and with a stated SPF value (ideally 30 or hi

videos.author: drspf
videos.caption: The sun shirt kids were right all along. 🤙 Sun protection is smart, evidence-based, and anti-aging- let’s not go backwards in our thinking s

her_coverage.summary: Very current: 'Sunscreen doesn't cause cancer' TT posted ~3d ago (174K), plus a bemotrizinol explainer and multiple sunscreen guides.

themes.corpus_position: “Sunscreen doesn’t cause cancer. The myth evolves from evidence that some chemical sunscreens get absorbed — absorption is not the same as harm.”

angles.angle: Myth-bust: sunscreen doesn't cause cancer — absorption ≠ harm

Diaper-rash cream is NOT sunscreen — say it plainly

links.label: her 'derm tips' short (nizoral clears tinea versicolor)

themes.corpus_position: “Fungal acne is actually a form of folliculitis caused by a common skin yeast (Malassezia).”

angles.angle: Name the summer rash: tinea versicolor, not sun spots
Same yeast as fungal acne — the Malassezia link
Treat it: antifungal wash + why it recurs in heat

more_topics.note: Small but a 1% breakout — her under-served hair/scalp lane (high ceiling).
Big raw views but big-account (x3.7) — format, not a small-creator wave.

her_wins.summary: Glass-skin routine is a follower magnet — superviral 1.60M on TT (♥ gold, top-1% engagement) + 1.33M on IG (♥ silver). Her biggest recent breakout.
her_wins.open_lane: Part 2 — the exfoliation step you teased, or the PM/night glass-skin routine (extend the series; it's generating follows)

stitch_shortlist.author: @tierra5239
stitch_shortlist.claim: UV cooling sleeves fully block sun damage

pitch.the_claim: Cooling arm sleeves 'protect skin from sun damage' — the summer must-have every commenter is buying.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 3.84M views at ~630K/day, peak-summer product wave with a checkable protection claim.
pitch.your_fit: Deep sun-protection corpus (sunscreen guides; 'sunscreen doesn't cause cancer' TT ~3d ago). Rate sleeves as real UPF vs marketing.
angles: 'yes, and' — only if it's rated UPF 50+, not just 'cooling'
empathy-first: sleeves are great sun protection when the fabric is tested
what actually blocks UV: tight weave, dark/rated fabric, coverage

pitch.the_claim: A 'PDRN + collagen' combo is the best fix for wrinkles and dryness / skin regeneration.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 884K at ~210K/day on a trend-ingredient combo; commenters asking 'does it actually work?'
pitch.your_fit: She rates ingredients bad-better-best; can give the real evidence level on PDRN and why topical collagen can't penetrate.
angles: myth-check: the collagen molecule is too big to absorb topically
PDRN (salmon DNA) — what the evidence does and doesn't show
save your money: what truly rebuilds collagen — retinoids + daily SPF

pitch.the_claim: Skip retinol and its 'uglies' — Ponds cold cream is better.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 854K at ~155K/day; a 'skip the actives' take that's classic derm myth-bust bait.
pitch.your_fit: She teaches retinoids constantly (tret + azelaic combining videos). Explain 'retinol uglies' = normal retinization.
angles: Ponds moisturizes; retinol remodels — different jobs
the 'retinol uglies' are temporary — here's how to ease them
switch-don't-quit: buffer retinol, don't drop it for cold cream

stitch_shortlist.author: @gorilla.health
stitch_shortlist.claim: Sunscreen chemicals absorb into your blood

pitch.the_claim: Cites the FDA maximal-use study to imply chemical sunscreen in your bloodstream is dangerous.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 204K at ~47K/day, and it names a REAL study — the pseudo-scientific version of the sunscreen scare.
pitch.your_fit: Exact match to her 'sunscreen doesn't cause cancer — absorption isn't harm' TT (~174K, 3d ago). Repeat with the study named.
angles: absorption is NOT harm — the FDA asked for data, not a warning
what the maximal-use study actually tested (and didn't find)
if it worries you, use mineral SPF — just wear something

pitch.the_claim: Paul Saladino (carnivore influencer, millions of followers): conventional sunscreen is a scam.
pitch.why_it_stitches: Dangerous, high-reach misinfo that punches UP; matches this week's anti-sunscreen wave. (Clip 81K but the account is huge.)
pitch.your_fit: Her 'sunscreen doesn't cause cancer' TT (3d) is the direct rebuttal; name the sunscreen paradox.
angles: empathy-first: I get the chemical fear — here's the actual data
'scam'? melanoma kills ~8,000 Americans a year
punches up: name the influencer, protect the follower

stitch_shortlist.author: @labmuffinbeautyscience
stitch_shortlist.claim: Diaper cream works as sunscreen (derm debate)

pitch.the_claim: Derm-vs-derm debate over whether diaper (zinc-oxide) paste can work as sunscreen.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 569 comments on 73K views (huge comment heat) and the diaper-cream-SPF myth is trending (drdrayzday too).
pitch.your_fit: Ties to the sun-protection theme; she can settle it — untested % zinc oxide is not labeled broad-spectrum SPF.
angles: settle it: diaper paste isn't tested or labeled as broad-spectrum SPF
yes-and: zinc-oxide % alone doesn't equal tested protection
why the myth spreads — and an actually-cheap SPF that works

pitch.the_claim: A chaotic multi-step routine going viral as comedy — no hard claim to correct.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 2.3M in-lane wave; react-to-the-routine only (aesthetic content, weak claim).
pitch.your_fit: She rates routines; can flag over-layering.
angles: as a derm: what I'd cut from this routine
over-layering actives = barrier damage

pitch.the_claim: A derm ranks retinoids by potency, says all are anti-aging long-term.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 2.1M in-lane derm claim — but Turkish-language; stitch the CLAIM, not the clip.
pitch.your_fit: Her retinoid content is deep; translate and confirm/nuance.
angles: translate + confirm: potency isn't 'better' for everyone
tolerability beats raw strength

more_stitches.author: @throttleconfessions
more_stitches.claim: 'Worst sunburn of my life' comedy skit (1.1M)

pitch.the_claim: Viral biker/couple skit about a brutal sunburn — no claim, pure comedy.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.1M sunburn wave = free PSA reach; ride it, don't correct it.
pitch.your_fit: Sunburn is her lane; turn the laugh into a teach.
angles: ride it: a burn like that = DNA damage + melanoma risk
what to do for a bad burn (and what not to)

pitch.the_claim: A civilian loudly DEFENDING chemical SPF 50 — a rare pro-sunscreen wave.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 906K at ~245K/day; amplify the correct take amid the anti-sunscreen noise.
pitch.your_fit: Aligns with her stance; boost, don't battle.
angles: yes-and: chemical filters are safe and elegant — here's why
signal-boost the good take instead of only fighting the bad

news.headline: Study’s own author rebuts viral ‘sunscreen quadruples melanoma’ claim
news.summary: The anti-sunscreen 'a study proves it causes cancer' myth is peaking; the study’s lead author publicly corrected the record — and she just posted on exactly this.
news.her_angle: Stitch the myth: the link is the sunscreen paradox (users tan more), not causation.
